title: Linting for Correctness, Safety, and Maintainability · Risk Brief: Bug Rules
summary: Configure a high-signal linter beyond default style warnings. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates bug rules increment in a risk-based lint policy and fixtures. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enable rules for invalid control flow, unsafe APIs, promises, hooks, and accessibility as applicable.
Start with maintained recommended rules and promote material findings to errors.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Build Bug Rules evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-bug-rules-1dnq5o: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Bug Rules into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-bug-rules-1dnq5o:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Bug Rules model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Bug Rules applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Enable rules for invalid control flow, unsafe APIs, promises, hooks, and accessibility as applicable.
